req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for daily inter-account transfer limit requirement.

Requirement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own
accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact
min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from datetime import date

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code interface assumed
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from banking.transfers import TransferService, TransferResult
# from banking.accounts import Account
# from banking.exceptions import (
#     TransferDeclinedException,
#     InsufficientFundsException,
#     InvalidTransferException,
# )
#
# The classes below are stubs so the test file is self-contained and runnable.
# Replace them with real imports once the production module exists.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DAILY_TRANSFER_LIMIT = Decimal("25000.00")


class InsufficientFundsException(Exception):
    pass


class TransferDeclinedException(Exception):
    """Raised when a transfer is declined (e.g. limit exceeded)."""
    pass


class InvalidTransferException(Exception):
    """Raised for structurally invalid transfer requests."""
    pass


# -- Minimal stub implementations so tests can run without production code ---

class Account:
    def __init__(self, account_id: str, owner_id: str, balance: Decimal):
        self.account_id = account_id
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.balance = balance


class TransferResult:
    def __init__(self, success: bool, amount: Decimal, reference: str = ""):
        self.success = success
        self.amount = amount
        self.reference = reference


class TransferService:
    """Stub – replace with real implementation."""

    def __init__(self, daily_limit: Decimal = DAILY_TRANSFER_LIMIT):
        self._daily_limit = daily_limit
        # Tracks {(owner_id, date): cumulative_transferred}
        self._daily_totals: dict[tuple, Decimal] = {}

    def _today(self) -> date:
        return date.today()

    def _get_daily_total(self, owner_id: str) -> Decimal:
        return self._daily_totals.get((owner_id, self._today()), Decimal("0"))

    def transfer(
        self,
        from_account: Account,
        to_account: Account,
        amount: Decimal,
    ) -> TransferResult:
        # --- validation ---
        if from_account.owner_id != to_account.owner_id:
            raise InvalidTransferException("Accounts must belong to the same customer.")
        if amount <= Decimal("0"):
            raise InvalidTransferException("Transfer amount must be positive.")
        if from_account.account_id == to_account.account_id:
            raise InvalidTransferException("Source and destination accounts must differ.")

        # --- limit check ---
        daily_total = self._get_daily_total(from_account.owner_id)
        projected_total = daily_total + amount

        if projected_total > self._daily_limit:
            raise TransferDeclinedException(
                f"Transfer of {amount} would exceed the daily limit of "
                f"{self._daily_limit}. Already transferred: {daily_total}."
            )

        # --- funds check ---
        if amount > from_account.balance:
            raise InsufficientFundsException("Insufficient funds.")

        # --- execute ---
        from_account.balance -= amount
        to_account.balance += amount
        self._daily_totals[(from_account.owner_id, self._today())] = projected_total

        return TransferResult(success=True, amount=amount, reference="TXN-STUB")


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture()
def service() -> TransferService:
    """Fresh TransferService with no prior daily activity."""
    return TransferService()


@pytest.fixture()
def owner_id() -> str:
    return "CUST-001"


@pytest.fixture()
def accounts(owner_id) -> tuple[Account, Account]:
    """A pair of accounts belonging to the same customer, well-funded."""
    source = Account("ACC-001", owner_id, balance=Decimal("500000.00"))
    destination = Account("ACC-002", owner_id, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
    return source, destination


@pytest.fixture()
def funded_accounts(owner_id) -> tuple[Account, Account]:
    """Alias for accounts – makes intent clearer in some tests."""
    source = Account("ACC-001", owner_id, balance=Decimal("500000.00"))
    destination = Account("ACC-002", owner_id, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
    return source, destination


# ===========================================================================
# Helper
# ===========================================================================

def _make_transfer(service, accounts, amount_str: str) -> TransferResult:
    source, destination = accounts
    return service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal(amount_str))


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Boundary – exact threshold (≤ limit)
# ===========================================================================

class TestExactDailyLimit:
    """Transfers that equal the daily limit exactly must be approved."""

    def test_single_transfer_equal_to_limit_is_approved(self, service, accounts):
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "25000.00")

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.amount == Decimal("25000.00")

    def test_multiple_transfers_summing_to_exact_limit_are_approved(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "10000.00")
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "10000.00")
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "5000.00")  # total = 25 000

        assert result.success is True

    def test_two_equal_transfers_summing_to_limit_are_approved(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "12500.00")
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "12500.00")  # total = 25 000

        assert result.success is True

    def test_balance_updated_correctly_after_limit_transfer(self, service, accounts):
        source, destination = accounts
        initial_source_balance = source.balance

        service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("25000.00"))

        assert source.balance == initial_source_balance - Decimal("25000.00")
        assert destination.balance == Decimal("25000.00")


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Boundary – one cent above the threshold (> limit)
# ===========================================================================

class TestJustAboveDailyLimit:
    """Transfers that exceed the daily limit by the smallest unit must be declined."""

    def test_single_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_is_declined(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "25000.01")

    def test_cumulative_transfers_one_cent_above_limit_is_declined(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "25000.00")  # use up the full limit
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.01")  # one cent more → declined

    def test_transfer_declined_does_not_update_balance(self, service, accounts):
        source, destination = accounts
        balance_before = source.balance

        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("25000.01"))

        assert source.balance == balance_before  # no funds moved

    def test_transfer_declined_does_not_update_daily_total(
        self, service, accounts, owner_id
    ):
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "25000.01")

        # After a declined attempt the daily total must remain 0
        assert service._get_daily_total(owner_id) == Decimal("0")


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Boundary – one cent below the threshold (just within limit)
# ===========================================================================

class TestJustBelowDailyLimit:
    """Transfers one cent below the limit must be approved."""

    def test_single_transfer_one_cent_below_limit_is_approved(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "24999.99")

        assert result.success is True

    def test_cumulative_transfer_one_cent_below_limit_is_approved(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "24999.98")
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.01")  # total = 24 999.99

        assert result.success is True


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Minimum transfer amount boundary
# ===========================================================================

class TestMinimumTransferAmount:
    """The smallest valid transfer is one cent ($0.01)."""

    def test_one_cent_transfer_is_approved(self, service, accounts):
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.01")

        assert result.success is True

    def test_zero_amount_transfer_is_rejected(self, service, accounts):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidTransferException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.00")

    def test_negative_amount_transfer_is_rejected(self, service, accounts):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidTransferException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "-0.01")


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Daily limit resets across calendar days
# ===========================================================================

class TestDailyLimitReset:
    """The $25,000 ceiling must reset at the start of each calendar day."""

    def test_limit_resets_on_next_calendar_day(self, service, accounts):
        source, destination = accounts

        # Day 1 – exhaust the limit
        with patch.object(service, "_today", return_value=date(2024, 1, 1)):
            service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("25000.00"))

        # Day 2 – full limit available again
        with patch.object(service, "_today", return_value=date(2024, 1, 2)):
            result = service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("25000.00"))

        assert result.success is True

    def test_declined_on_same_day_after_full_limit_used(self, service, accounts):
        source, destination = accounts

        with patch.object(service, "_today", return_value=date(2024, 6, 15)):
            service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("25000.00"))
            with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
                service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("0.01"))


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Cumulative daily tracking
# ===========================================================================

class TestCumulativeDailyTracking:
    """Multiple transfers within the same day share a single daily counter."""

    def test_many_small_transfers_accumulate_toward_limit(self, service, accounts):
        # 250 transfers of $100 = $25,000 total
        for _ in range(250):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "100.00")

        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.01")

    def test_remaining_allowance_is_correct_after_partial_use(
        self, service, accounts, owner_id
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "15000.00")

        daily_used = service._get_daily_total(owner_id)
        remaining = DAILY_TRANSFER_LIMIT - daily_used

        assert remaining == Decimal("10000.00")

    def test_transfer_exactly_consuming_remaining_allowance_is_approved(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "15000.00")
        result = _make_transfer(service, accounts, "10000.00")  # uses up remainder

        assert result.success is True

    def test_transfer_exceeding_remaining_allowance_is_declined(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "15000.00")
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "10000.01")


# ===========================================================================
# 7. Large transfers well above the limit
# ===========================================================================

class TestLargeTransfersAboveLimit:
    """Transfers far above the limit must also be declined cleanly."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        "25001.00",
        "50000.00",
        "100000.00",
        "999999.99",
    ])
    def test_transfer_well_above_limit_is_declined(self, service, accounts, amount):
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, amount)


# ===========================================================================
# 8. Transfers between accounts of different customers
# ===========================================================================

class TestCrossCustomerTransferRejected:
    """The daily-limit feature is scoped to same-owner transfers only.
    Cross-customer transfers must be rejected as invalid regardless of amount."""

    def test_transfer_to_different_owner_account_is_rejected(self, service):
        source = Account("ACC-001", "CUST-001", Decimal("50000.00"))
        foreign_destination = Account("ACC-999", "CUST-002", Decimal("0.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InvalidTransferException):
            service.transfer(source, foreign_destination, Decimal("1000.00"))


# ===========================================================================
# 9. Same source and destination account
# ===========================================================================

class TestSameAccountTransferRejected:
    """Transferring from an account to itself must be rejected."""

    def test_same_account_transfer_is_rejected(self, service, owner_id):
        account = Account("ACC-001", owner_id, Decimal("50000.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InvalidTransferException):
            service.transfer(account, account, Decimal("100.00"))


# ===========================================================================
# 10. Insufficient funds (independent of daily limit)
# ===========================================================================

class TestInsufficientFunds:
    """A transfer within the daily limit but beyond the account balance
    must be declined for insufficient funds."""

    def test_transfer_within_limit_but_exceeding_balance_is_declined(
        self, service, owner_id
    ):
        source = Account("ACC-001", owner_id, balance=Decimal("500.00"))
        destination = Account("ACC-002", owner_id, balance=Decimal("0.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsException):
            service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("500.01"))

    def test_transfer_exactly_equal_to_balance_is_approved(
        self, service, owner_id
    ):
        source = Account("ACC-001", owner_id, balance=Decimal("500.00"))
        destination = Account("ACC-002", owner_id, balance=Decimal("0.00"))

        result = service.transfer(source, destination, Decimal("500.00"))

        assert result.success is True
        assert source.balance == Decimal("0.00")


# ===========================================================================
# 11. Precision / decimal edge cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestDecimalPrecision:
    """Monetary arithmetic must be exact; floating-point rounding must not
    cause a compliant transfer to be incorrectly declined or vice-versa."""

    def test_limit_enforced_with_sub_cent_precision_not_accepted(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        """Only two decimal places (cents) are meaningful for USD."""
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            # Amount with more than 2 decimal places should be handled
            # (either rounded or rejected – adapt to production behaviour).
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.001")

    def test_repeated_fractional_transfers_do_not_drift_past_limit(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        # 25 000 transfers of $1.00 each – must hit the ceiling exactly.
        for _ in range(25000):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "1.00")

        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "0.01")


# ===========================================================================
# 12. Error message quality
# ===========================================================================

class TestDeclinedTransferErrorMessages:
    """Declined transfers must surface a clear, informative error message."""

    def test_exception_message_references_daily_limit(self, service, accounts):
        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException, match="25000"):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "25000.01")

    def test_exception_message_references_amount_already_transferred(
        self, service, accounts
    ):
        _make_transfer(service, accounts, "20000.00")

        with pytest.raises(TransferDeclinedException, match="20000"):
            _make_transfer(service, accounts, "5000.01")
```

---

## Design notes

| Concern | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Monetary type** | `Decimal` throughout – avoids floating-point drift that could silently pass or fail boundary checks. |
| **Boundary strategy** | Each boundary is covered at *exact*, *−1 cent*, and *+1 cent* — the classic on-point / off-point pair. |
| **Daily reset** | `unittest.mock.patch` freezes `date.today()` so the test controls the calendar without sleeping or touching the system clock. |
| **Stub vs real code** | The stub `TransferService` is intentionally minimal. Replace the import lines at the top once the production module exists; every test should still pass against a compliant implementation. |
| **Independence** | Each test creates its own `service` fixture instance so daily-total state never leaks between tests. |
| **Parametrize** | Used for the "well above limit" group to avoid repetitive boilerplate while keeping individual failure messages clear. |

```python
"""
Pytest tests for daily inter-account transfer limit requirement.

Requirement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own
accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact
min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from datetime import date

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code interface assumed
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from banking.transfers import TransferService, TransferResult
# from banking.accounts import Account
# from banking.exceptions import (
#     TransferDeclinedException,
#     InsufficientFundsException,
#     InvalidTransferException,
# )
#
# The classes below are stubs so the test file is self-contained and runnable.
# Replace them with real imports once the production module exists.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DAILY_TRANSFER_LIMIT = Decimal("25000.00")


class InsufficientFundsException(Exception):
    pass


class TransferDeclinedException(Exception):
    """Raised when a transfer is declined (e.g. limit exceeded)."""
    pass


class InvalidTransferException(Exception):
    """Raised for structurally invalid transfer requests."""
    pass


# -- Minimal stub implementations so tests can run without production code ---

class Account:
    def __init__(self, account_id: str, owner_id: str, balance: Decimal):
        self.account_id = account_id
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.balance = balance


class TransferResult:
    def __init__(self, success: bool, amount: Decimal, reference: str = ""):
        self.success = success
        self.amount = amount
        self.reference = reference


class TransferService:
    """Stub – replace with real implementation."""

    def __init__(self, daily_limit: Decimal = DAILY_TRANSFER_LIMIT):
        self._daily_limit = daily_limit
        # Tracks {(owner_id, date): cumulative_transferred}
        self._daily_totals: dict[tuple, Decimal] = {}

    def _today(self) -> date:
        return date.today()

    def _get_daily_total(self, owner_id: str) -> Decimal:
        return self._daily_totals.get((owner_id, self._today()), Decimal("0"))

    def transfer(
        self,
        from_account: Account,
        to_account: Account,
        amount: Decimal,
    ) -> TransferResult:
        # --- validation ---
        if from_account.owner_id != to_account.owner_id:
            raise InvalidTransferException("Accounts must belong to the same customer.")
        if amount <= Decimal("0"):
            raise InvalidTransferException("Transfer amount must be positive.")
        if from_account.account_id == to_account.account_id:
            raise InvalidTransferException("Source and destination accounts must differ.")

        # --- limit check ---
        daily_total = self._get_daily_total(from_account.owner_id)
        projected_total = daily_total + amount

        if projected_total > self._daily_limit:
            raise TransferDeclinedException(
                f"Transfer of {amount} would exceed the daily limit of "
                f"{self._daily_limit}. Already transferred: {daily_total}."
            )

        # --- funds check ---
        if amount > from_account.balance:
            raise InsufficientFundsException("Insufficient funds.")

        # --- execute ---
        from_account.balance -= amount
        to_account.balance += amount
        self._daily_totals[(from_account.owner_id, self._today())] = projected_total

        return TransferResult(success=True, amount=amount, reference="TXN-STUB")


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture()
def service() -> TransferService:
    """Fresh TransferService
